---
title: "svmForayCAPSTONE"
format: html
---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(yardstick)
library(vip)
library(LiblineaR)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
```


Assumes we have `full_data` from loadFromPython

```{r}
# # this is to check no information rate:
# full_data %>%
#   group_by(genre) %>%
#   summarise(count = n()) %>%
#   mutate(percentage = (count / sum(count)) * 100)
```


Train and test splits!!!

```{r}
filenames <- full_data %>% 
  select(genre:file) %>% 
  group_by(file) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup()

# filenames

set.seed(01110001+15) # "q" in binary plus 15
split <- initial_split(filenames, prop = 0.9, strata = genre)

train_data <- training(split)
test_data  <- testing(split)

train_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = genre)) +
  geom_bar()

test_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = genre)) +
  geom_bar() +
  ylim(c(0, 10))
```


```{r}
# filter training data as singingSvmData :D

full_svm_data <- full_data %>% 
  select(!label) %>% select(!t)
  
singingSvmData <- full_svm_data %>% 
  filter(file %in% train_data$file) 

singingSvmData_TEST <- full_svm_data %>% 
  filter(file %in% test_data$file)

# singingSvmData
```


```{r}
svm_spec <- svm_rbf(
  cost = tune(),
  rbf_sigma = tune()
) %>% 
  set_engine("kernlab") %>% 
  set_mode("classification")
```

```{r}
svm_recipe <- recipe(genre ~ ., data = singingSvmData) %>%
  step_rm(file) %>% 
  step_normalize(all_predictors())  # Centers and scales predictors

  # step_classdist(all_numeric_predictors(), class = "genre", pool = T) # this line specifies Mahalanobis distance, following Mandel & Ellis (2005)

# this is just cuz of how we're tuning
svm_recipe_full <- recipe(genre ~ ., data = full_svm_data) %>%
  step_rm(file) %>% 
  step_normalize(all_predictors())  # Centers and scales predictors
```

```{r}
# svm_workflow <- workflow() %>%
#   add_recipe(svm_recipe) %>%
#   add_model(svm_spec)

svm_workflow_full <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(svm_recipe_full) %>%
  add_model(svm_spec)
```


```{r}
# Here we use 5-fold CV on the whole dataset rather than just the train split, since we're just tuning stuff and not actually training our final model. Maybe that's dumb. Idrc.

# !! CAREFUL THIS CELL TAKES ONE HUNDRED YEARS!!

# svm_params <- parameters(
#   cost(range = c(-5L, 5L)),
#   rbf_sigma(range = c(-10L, 0L))
# )
# 
# svm_grid <- grid_regular(svm_params, levels = 5)
# 
# 
# set.seed(222)
# 
# svm_tune <- svm_workflow_full %>%
#   tune_grid(
#     resamples = vfold_cv(full_svm_data, v = 5),
#     grid = svm_grid,
#     metrics = metric_set(f_meas)
#   )

```

```{r}
svm_tune %>% 
  autoplot() + 
  scale_x_continuous()

best_svm_params <- select_best(svm_tune, metric = "f_meas")
# best_svm_params

svm_tune %>% 
  collect_metrics() %>% 
  filter(cost == best_svm_params$cost[1]) %>% 
  filter(rbf_sigma == best_svm_params$rbf_sigma[1])
```

The tuning process found that cost 32, rbf_sigma = 0.003162278. that gave CV f_meas 0.3567638, std_err = 0.003711886. Rad!!! (well not rad, very bad, but whatever.)



# Final fit stuff (accuracy, F1, conf. mat.)

```{r}
# Finalize workflow:
svm_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(svm_recipe) %>%
  add_model(finalize_model(svm_spec, best_svm_params))

# could also manually set cost = 32 and rbf_sigma = 0.003162278 in the event of data wipage. because there's NO WORLD where we run that tuning cell again, jeez.
```


```{r}
final_fit <- fit(svm_workflow, data = singingSvmData)
```

```{r}
# in-sample final predictions
in_sample_pred <- final_fit %>% 
  predict(new_data = singingSvmData)
  

in_sample_pred <- in_sample_pred %>% 
  cbind(singingSvmData)

in_sample_pred
```

```{r}
# vowel-level in-sample accuracy and conf mat:
in_sample_pred %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class)

in_sample_pred %>% 
  f_meas(genre, .pred_class)

in_sample_pred %>% 
  conf_mat(truth = genre, estimate = .pred_class) %>% 
  autoplot(cm, type = "heatmap") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fccf90", high = "#cc8016") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
#| message: false
# word-level stuff (in-sample still)

in_sample_word_pred <- in_sample_pred %>% 
  group_by(file, genre, .pred_class) %>% 
  count() %>% 
  group_by(file) %>% 
  arrange(desc(n), .by_group = T) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(!n)

in_sample_word_pred %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class)

in_sample_word_pred %>% 
  group_by(genre) %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class) %>%
  select(!.metric:.estimator)

in_sample_word_pred %>% 
  f_meas(genre, .pred_class)

in_sample_word_pred %>% 
  conf_mat(truth = genre, estimate = .pred_class) %>% 
  autoplot(cm, type = "heatmap") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fccf90", high = "#cc8016") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


Time for test data stuff!!

```{r}
# in-sample final predictions
outof_sample_pred <- final_fit %>% 
  predict(new_data = singingSvmData_TEST)
  

outof_sample_pred <- outof_sample_pred %>% 
  cbind(singingSvmData_TEST)

outof_sample_pred
```

```{r}
# vowel-level accuracy and conf mat:
outof_sample_pred %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class)

outof_sample_pred %>% 
  f_meas(genre, .pred_class)

outof_sample_pred %>% 
  conf_mat(truth = genre, estimate = .pred_class) %>% 
  autoplot(cm, type = "heatmap") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fccf90", high = "#cc8016") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
outof_sample_word_pred <- outof_sample_pred %>% 
  group_by(file, genre, .pred_class) %>% 
  count() %>% 
  group_by(file) %>% 
  arrange(desc(n), .by_group = T) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(!n)

outof_sample_word_pred %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class)

outof_sample_word_pred %>% 
  group_by(genre) %>% 
  accuracy(genre, .pred_class) %>% 
  select(!.metric:.estimator)

outof_sample_word_pred %>% 
  f_meas(genre, .pred_class)

outof_sample_word_pred %>% 
  conf_mat(truth = genre, estimate = .pred_class) %>% 
  autoplot(cm, type = "heatmap") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fccf90", high = "#cc8016") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```



```{r}
outof_sample_pred %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = .pred_class, y = QR, color = .pred_class)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(legend.position="none")

outof_sample_pred %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = .pred_class, y = H1H2c, color = .pred_class)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(legend.position="none")

outof_sample_pred %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = .pred_class, y = CPP, color = .pred_class)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(legend.position="none")
```















# old



```{r}
in_sample_confusion <- final_fit %>%
  augment(new_data = singingSvmData) %>% 
  conf_mat(truth = genre, estimate = .pred_class)

in_sample_confusion

genre_label = rep(colnames(in_sample_confusion$table), ncol(in_sample_confusion$table))
in_sample_confusion %>% 
  autoplot() +    
  aes(fill = genre_label) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#000000", "darkgray", "red", "#7570B3", "lightgreen", "blue", "#E6AB02", "brown", "#66A61E"))
```



```{r}
# Dig into the tidymodels object to extract the raw LiblineaR engine
engine_fit <- extract_fit_engine(final_fit)

# Extract the weights matrix (W)
# The columns are your features, and the last column ('Bias') is the intercept.
# cat("\n--- Underlying SVM Weights ---\n")
# weights
# If you want to format it nicely into a dataframe:
weights <- engine_fit$W
(weights_df <- as.data.frame(weights))

# 1. Grab the row names (which are your class levels) before pivoting
weights_df <- as.data.frame(weights) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Class")

# 2. Pivot the data, but keep the 'Class' column intact
plot_data <- weights_df %>%
  select(-Bias) %>% # Still dropping the intercept
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Class, # Pivot all columns EXCEPT Class
    names_to = "Feature", 
    values_to = "Weight"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Direction = ifelse(Weight > 0, "Positive", "Negative"))
# 
# # my_custom_order <- c("CQ", "SQ", "Contact Duration", "Decontact Duration", "PIC", "PDC", "H1-H2c", "H2-H4c", "H1-A1c", "H1-A2c", "H1-A3c", "F0", "CPP", "Energy")
# 
# my_custom_order <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3", "PC4", "CQ", "SQ", "Contact Duration", "Decontact Duration", "PIC", "PDC", "H1-H2c", "H2-H4c", "H1-A1c", "H1-A2c", "H1-A3c", "F0", "CPP", "Energy")
# 
# plot_data <- plot_data %>% 
#   mutate(Feature = factor(
#     case_match(
#       Feature,
#       "CQ_H" ~ "CQ",
#       "SQ" ~ "SQ",
#       "SQ2_SQ1" ~ "Contact Duration",
#       "SQ4_SQ3" ~ "Decontact Duration",
#       "peak_Vel" ~ "PIC",
#       "min_Vel" ~ "PDC",
#       "H1H2c" ~ "H1-H2c",
#       "H2H4c" ~ "H2-H4c",
#       "H1A1c" ~ "H1-A1c",
#       "H1A2c" ~ "H1-A2c",
#       "H1A3c" ~ "H1-A3c",
#       "strF0" ~ "F0",
#       "CPP" ~ "CPP",
#       "Energy" ~ "Energy",
#       "PC1" ~ "PC1",
#       "PC2" ~ "PC2",
#       "PC3" ~ "PC3",
#       "PC4" ~ "PC4"
#     ), 
#     levels = rev(my_custom_order)
#   ))

# --- STEP 1: DEFINE CUSTOM Y-AXIS ORDER ---
# List your features exactly as you want them to read from TOP to BOTTOM


# Convert to factor and reverse the order so the first item plots at the top
# plot_data$Feature <- factor(plot_data$Feature, levels = rev(my_custom_order))

```


```{r fig.height=15, fig.width=10}


# --- STEP 2: PLOT THE DATA ---
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Weight, y = Feature, fill = Direction)) +
  
  # Use geom_col_pattern instead of geom_col
  geom_col_pattern(
    aes(pattern = Direction),
    color = "black",            # Adds a black outline to the bars
    pattern_fill = "black",     # Color of the pattern (dots/stripes)
    pattern_density = 0.1,      # How dense the pattern is
    pattern_spacing = 0.05      # Space between pattern lines/dots
  ) + 
  
  # Set the patterns (circle = dotted, stripe = striped)
  scale_pattern_manual(values = c("Positive" = "stripe", "Negative" = "circle")) +
  
  # Change the base fill to colorblind/B&W friendly grays
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Positive" = "gray77", "Negative" = "gray45")) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Class, scales = "free_x") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  
  # --- STEP 3: CONTROL TEXT SIZES ---
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # Global text size baseline
    text = element_text(size = 12),              
    
    # Specific text element controls (adjust numbers as needed)
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), # "Weight" and "Feature"
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),                 # The actual numbers/feature names
    strip.text = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")  # The facet labels above each panel
  ) +

  labs(title = "← Negative                         Positive →") +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")
  )
```
